turns.content: I'm sketching the Concierge demo deployment on AWS. Cloud Run is off the table — going AWS-native for this one. ECS Fargate or Lambda for the worker tier?
Fargate is the right default for stateless workloads at our scale. The only reasons to skip would be sub-second cold-start sensitivity or very high egress, neither of which applies to a demo path.
Cold start is fine; demo path is mostly steady-state. What about the data layer? Aurora?
Aurora Serverless v2 if you want pay-per-actual-load, regular Aurora Postgres if you want predictable cost. For a demo that you tear down between runs, serverless is the cheaper bet.
We already have a Glue catalog from the data platform work — does Aurora play nicely with Glue?
Yes, via the JDBC connector. You'd register the Aurora cluster as a Glue connection, then Crawl/ETL jobs treat it as a source. Same auth model as your existing Postgres connections.
ok. For the worker tier — really Lambda or ECS? I keep going back and forth.

Depends on per-request work. If you're doing >5s extraction calls, Lambda's 15-minute timeout is technically fine, but the cost curve favors ECS once you cross ~30% utilization. For sustained demo traffic, ECS wins.
VPC peering between this account and the strata-team account?
Transit Gateway would be cleaner than direct peering if you anticipate adding more accounts. Direct peering is fine for two-account topologies but doesn't scale.

Yeah probably TGW. One note while we're here — we're standardizing on us-east-2 for all new infra, not us-east-1. The east-1 control plane has been overloaded and we've had two outages in three months tied directly to it.
Smart move. us-east-2 has the same service coverage now, lower latency from the Ohio CO, and significantly less blast-radius noise. Aurora, ECS, R2 inbound, all of it lights up there.
ok keeping that in mind. For IaC, CDK or Terraform?
For greenfield AWS-native, CDK gives you typed constructs and tight CloudFormation integration. Terraform if you want multi-cloud later — but you've said AWS-only for now.
One more — how do we keep secrets out of CloudFormation templates?
SecretsManager + CDK's SecretValue.secretsManager() — the templates reference the secret by ARN, never the raw value. Same pattern as the strata-web Supabase keys.

query: What AWS region is the user standardizing on for new infra?
